title: How to Turn Your Habits Into a System That Actually Works
description:
Your habits feel consistent but your big goals feel stuck. The problem isn't willpower — it's that your habits are disconnected. Here's how I built a system where habits feed each other and create real momentum.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

In this video, I break down:
- Why isolated habits fail (even when you're consistent)
- The three-category structure that makes habits connect (foundation, work, reflection)
- How to use feedback loops to see which habits actually move the needle
- Why a well-built system requires LESS discipline, not more
- The iteration mindset that turns a system into an engine

This isn't about optimizing every minute. It's about building architecture that makes your real goals more likely.

[LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]
thumbnail_concept: Split-screen visual: left side shows scattered icons (meditation, reading, exercise, work) in visual chaos; right side shows the same icons connected with arrows forming a smooth loop or engine diagram. Text overlay reads 'Habits → Engine' in bold sans-serif. Color mood: warm and professional, slightly energetic, not clickbait.
end_screen_suggestion: Link to related video on feedback loops or a playlist on systems-based personal growth.
